key_facts: Established June 2022 as wholly-owned subsidiary of Yuexiu Capital (Guangzhou city SASAC)
2024 new distributed installations: 10.60 GW (primary rural/farmer segment)
Managed PV capacity: 14.08 GW by end-2024
CNY 161B (1,610亿) new energy investment in 2023; plans CNY 200B over 3 years
State-backed: controlled by Guangzhou city government via Yuexiu Capital

funding_history.notes: Capitalized by Guangzhou city SASAC via Yuexiu Capital; uses state credit rating for cheap capital

business_model_notes: State-backed financial capital entering distributed solar via purpose-built leasing vehicle. Yuexiu acts as the developer and long-term owner of distributed PV assets installed on farmer rooftops. Revenue: electricity sales (PPA with grid or merchant), government FiT, O&M service fees. Strategy: deploy massive state capital at scale (10+ GW/year) to capture rural distributed solar asset ownership before market consolidates. This is state capitalism responding to carbon neutrality pledge (2020) and整县推进 (2021).
competitive_position: Fastest-growing entity in financing/leasing segment. State capital cost advantage (AAA parent) vs private competitors. 14.08 GW managed by end-2024 makes it larger than most independent O&M companies by managed capacity. 450K+ farmers served rivals Chint Anneng's 2M user base trajectory.
research_notes: High confidence. Annual report figures from OFWeek Solar (June 2024) and CLBA. The explosive growth (673% revenue, 1,279% profit YoY) reflects the 2022 startup year as base — meaningful absolute numbers are the 14.08 GW managed and CNY 2.734B revenue which are large in absolute terms. Confirmed state-SASAC ownership via public corporate records.
